item.title: How China is charging forward with EV adoption as Canada prepares to welcome its cars
item.description: As Canada opens the door to Chinese electric vehicles following a trade deal earlier this year, it’s leaving space for China's EV innovation to enter, too. While some say this comes with big rewards in terms of boosting EV uptake and infrastructure in Canada, others are raising concerns over the threat to North American automakers and data security.

item.title: Public servant says she's 'collateral damage' as government prepares to test new pay system
item.description: A longtime federal public servant says she’s become "collateral damage" as her department clears its backlog of Phoenix payroll issues so it can test replacement software — and in doing so introduced an error to her file, refused to fix it and is now clawing back hundreds of dollars per paycheque.

item.title: Government looks to prorogue Parliament if Liberals sweep April 13 by-elections
item.description: Move would give the party control of House of Commons committees, and allow it to push through legislation more easily

item.title: Toronto’s newest transit project gets green light with provincial, federal funding
item.description: Video

item.title: Refugee tribunal ruled on more than 45,000 cases since 2019 without in-person hearings
item.description: Conservative immigration critics write to minister calling on her to bring an end to asylum decisions based on paperwork alone

item.title: Ontario is cracking down on fare evasion on GO Transit. Here are the new steeper fines being proposed
item.description: Video

item.title: Carney minister says trade talks with Trump administration have resumed
item.description: But Dominic LeBlanc, the Canadian minister responsible for bilateral talks, declined to offer details on what progress, if any, has been made.

item.title: Mark Carney sidesteps question on forced labour in China, says some regions require more ‘diligence’
Mark Carney is open to Doug Ford’s ‘vision’ for Billy Bishop expansion
Mark Carney, Doug Ford to cut development charges by up to 50 per cent to boost housing construction
Rainy forecast to dampen Toronto’s sunny weather, with possible thunderstorms overnight
Carney isn’t saying whether Michael Ma faces fallout for China remarks
Canada not worried U.S.-Mexico trade talks will upend trilateral deal, LeBlanc says
Leblanc says he’s confident Mexico will not quit USMCA for separate deal with U.S.
item.description: Trade minister brushed aside reports that Ottawa is lagging Mexico City in negotiations over renewing trade pact

item.title: ‘Historic agreement:’ Feds, Ontario invest $8.8 B to cut development charges in half
item.description: The federal and provincial governments will jointly spend $8.8 billion to lower municipal development charges that drive up housing costs over the next decade, Prime Minister Mark Carney and Premier Doug Ford announced Monday.

item.title: U.S. tariff talks suspended by Trump have resumed ‘in a sense’: LeBlanc
item.description: Canada-U.S. Trade Minister Dominic LeBlanc said talks with U.S. counterparts 'remain productive and we remain engaged. That is different than October.'

item.title: A long-awaited LRT to serve Toronto’s booming eastern waterfront is finally getting funding
item.description: Premier Doug Ford, Prime Minister Mark Carney and Mayor Olivia Chow made the joint announcement about the Waterfront East LRT Monday.

item.title: Man in critical condition after apartment shooting near Moss Park
Man charged after carjacking leads to Hwy. 401 collision in Etobicoke
Higher fines coming for fare evaders on GO Transit
item.description: Taking aim at GO Transit fare evaders, Premier Doug Ford’s government is proposing dramatically increased fines of up to $500.

item.title: Politics Insider: Air Canada CEO to step down
item.description: Plus: Finance Minister François-Philippe Champagne leads trade-diversification mission to China this week

item.title: Can the U.S. wrest Strait of Hormuz from Iran, a regional military power?
item.description: Thousands of soldiers from the U.S. Army's elite 82nd Airborne Division have started arriving in the Middle East, Reuters reported on Monday.

item.title: Avi Lewis vows to focus on cost of living and dismisses division within the NDP
2026 FIFA World Cup will make Toronto even busier. Here’s how the city plans to keep people moving
Carney attends fundraiser co-hosted by MP who cast doubt on Chinese human rights abuses
item.description: PM says Michael Ma apologized, Canada has strong measures to stop goods made with forced labour from entering the country

item.title: Can Avi Lewis make the federal NDP relevant again — without undermining its provincial cousins?
item.description: Avi Lewis, a former television host, journalist and documentarian, promises to make the federal NDP relevant again. But the first question for his leadership is whether he'll do so at the expense of the NDP's provincial wings.

item.title: Scarborough youth crime prevention programs get a last-minute reprieve from city council
Scarborough set to lose two critical youth crime prevention programs: ‘This will be history repeating itself’
Canadian authorities censoring European Parliament member Rima Hassan, Montreal organizations say
item.description: Community organizations claim Hassan was prevented from entering the country after being invited to speak at two conferences in Montreal

item.title: NDP Leader Avi Lewis says 'door is open' to Prairie leaders opposed to his energy policies
item.description: Avi Lewis spent much of his first news conference as federal NDP leader downplaying divisions with provincial wings of the party, as disagreements over energy policy threaten to overshadow his task of rebuilding from a disastrous election.

item.title: Carney defends Canada's approach to forced labour amid fallout from floor-crossing MP's comments
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney defended Canada's efforts to keep products made with forced labour out of the supply chain as one of his newest MPs continues to face backlash for appearing to cast doubt on the practice in China.

item.title: Carney says forced labour a ‘global issue,’ ‘parts of China’ at higher risk
item.description: Earlier this year United Nations experts said 'there is a persistent pattern of alleged State-imposed forced labour involving ethnic minorities across multiple provinces in China.'

item.title: Most Canadians support banning social media for those under 16: survey
Federal government, Ontario sign $8.8B deal to reduce development charges
item.description: Mark Carney and Doug Ford were among the politicians at a major event Monday morning to unveil the overhaul of how new housing and its infrastructure are paid for in Ontario.

item.title: Liberals dismiss fact-checking petition proposal for House of Commons
item.description: Federico Sanchez initiated an electronic petition to propose legislation that would help correct the record when MPs stray from the truth intentionally or are ill-informed.

item.title: Althia Raj: Avi Lewis inherited a divided and weakened NDP. Here’s how he plans to turn it around
item.description: The new NDP leader wants to see the Canadian government dream big, harkening back to FDR's New Deal. "People don't have faith in the governmen…

item.title: Tens of thousands are expected to live on Toronto’s booming waterfront. Jets at Billy Bishop may change all that
item.description: Though details are still scarce, plans to bring jets to the island airport could dampen demand for waterfront homes — and reduce the number th…

item.title: Finance minister to visit China this week as Canada aims to forge closer ties
item.description: Finance Minister François-Philippe Champagne is visiting China this week, a trip his office said will build on Prime Minister Mark Carney's visit to Canada's second-largest trading partner earlier this year.

item.title: Ford, Carney announce $8.8B to help cut development charges, spur housing builds in Ontario
item.description: The federal and provincial governments will each spend $4.4 billion on housing-related infrastructure over the next 10 years. The majority of the funding is intended to help cover those infrastructure costs for municipalities that lower development charges.

item.title: Avi Lewis is the new NDP leader, as Alberta, Sask. leaders say his policies can ‘hurt’
Air Canada CEO Michael Rousseau to retire later this year following language controversy
item.description: Air ‌Canada CEO Michael ​Rousseau will retire ​sometime in the fall after nearly ⁠two ⁠decades ‌with the airline, it said on Monday, a week after he made headlines by issuing an English-only message of condolence following the crash of Flight 8646 in New York.

item.title: Avi Lewis elected NDP leader after successful populist, left-wing campaign
Lewis begins NDP leadership with Prairie tension and a big rebuilding task ahead
item.description: Avi Lewis begins his term with the task of rebuilding the NDP in his unapologetically progressive vision, but there is already resistance from Alberta and Saskatchewan.

item.title: Canadian banks, pension funds have provided billions in financing to ICE contractors, advocacy group says
item.description: Palantir, General Dynamics and L3Harris among companies that have received investment totalling about $35-billion

item.title: Finance Minister to visit China on trade-diversification mission
item.description: Champagne will meet senior Chinese finance and banking officials during a visit from April 1 to 4

item.title: Online gambling is everywhere. Except Quebec
item.description: The post Online gambling is everywhere. Except Quebec appeared first on The Logic .

item.title: High-speed rail project connecting Toronto and Quebec City sparks backlash over cost, land use
item.description: Eastern Ontario residents and local leaders warn the multibillion-dollar project threatens communities and offers few benefits to those footing the bill

item.title: A major immigration reform bill is now law in Canada. Some worry it rolls back refugee rights
item.description: After a week that saw a scathing auditor general's report find the federal Immigration Department has failed to act on fraud in its international student program, a major bill reforming immigration powers is now law in Canada.

item.title: 'Light also exists': Some Cubans see hope in the darkness of the moment
item.description: The U.S. oil blockade has pushed Cuba into a third month of energy crisis, deepening daily hardship in a country already strained by crumbling infrastructure. But amid the darkness, some Cubans still see hope for a better future.

item.title: New NDP Leader Avi Lewis faces a complex political dynamic in rebuilding party
item.description: Provincial counterparts had mixed reactions to his decisive first-ballot victory

item.title: NDP leadership vote recap: Avi Lewis talks Canada’s role in Iran, wealth tax and ‘care economy,’ takes flak from NDP leaders in the Prairies
Nearly 82,000 potholes repaired in Toronto after third blitz in the city
item.description: The city said it was receiving pothole hotspot reports from residents, and that crews usually respond to reports within four days.

item.title: Lewis’ election as NDP leader divides party’s Western base
item.description: While the election of Avi Lewis as the new federal NDP leader has received mixed response, Lewis says he'll focus on strengthening the grassroots before seeking elected office.

item.title: Provincial supervisor announces first round of cuts at Toronto Catholic school board. Here’s what is targeted
item.description: Teachers and ex-board chair are upset as international language instruction and an intensive reading support program are two of the areas targeted.

item.title: The NDP has a new leader. What does Avi Lewis's arrival mean for the party?
item.description: Newly named NDP leader Avi Lewis promised to bring back the party from the political wilderness after its dismal showing in last year's federal election.

item.title: European Parliament member says she was denied entry to Canada due to pro-Palestinian views
item.description: Rima Hassan claims she was ‘prevented’ from entering the country, describing it as an attempt at censorship

item.title: Avi Lewis vows ‘NDP comeback starts now’ after winning leadership race
item.description: Avi Lewis received more than 50 per cent of the total votes cast during the NDP leadership convention, giving him the victory on the first ballot.

item.title: Jewish advocacy group presses Ottawa to boost community security spending
item.description: Centre for Israel and Jewish Affairs wrote to finance minister ahead of spring economic statement after report warned of possible extremist attack

item.title: Avi Lewis wins NDP leadership race in decisive first-ballot victory
As prime minister, Mark Carney makes Question Period a low priority
item.description: A Global News analysis of Hansard shows Prime Minister Mark Carney has attended Question Period less than 30 per cent of the time, much less than either of his two predecessors.

item.title: Ottawa's big bet on world's largest cricket farm ran into a simple problem: the 'yuck factor'
item.description: Ottawa backed the world's largest cricket farm with tens of millions in public money. It collapsed before reaching full production, exposing a gap between hype and demand while also leaving questions about how much of that money was ever recovered.

item.title: Federal NDP leadership candidates make final pitches at convention
item.description: New Democrats have been marred by existential crisis after losing official status in House of Commons

item.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford heading to Texas to talk trade and tariffs
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford will push a pro-trade and anti-tariff message during a multi-day trip to Texas, U.S. where he will meet Governor Greg Abbott, an ally of President Donald Trump, and business leaders.

item.title: Nate Erskine-Smith urges Liberal rivals to step aside in Scarborough Southwest byelection
Ontario’s budget banks on a housing resale rebound in 2026 — but real estate players aren’t so sure
Mark Carney’s strategy for the public service becomes clearer
item.description: It’s not a public-service overhaul, it’s a workaround

item.title: Bill C-12 curbing asylum, immigration rights becomes law amid warnings of court challenges
item.description: Law fast-tracks deportation for some asylum seekers and allows Ottawa to share immigration information with provinces
